_comment: Act 2 / Challenge 2.1 — The Loop That Won't Stop (combat + sub-GHz timing). Loaded by beacon/sim at /spiffs/challenge_2_1.json (or referenced by id in sim).
name: The Loop That Won't Stop

jam._comment: Sub-GHz signal jam phase. Badge must transmit stop_code within timing_window_ms.
jam._relay_note: When badge has no subghz cap, beacon acts as relay: badge sends MERCHANT_INPUT {phase:'jam_relay'} and beacon transmits the stop code itself, then ACKs via CHALLENGE_RESULT {jammed:true}.

combat._comment: RNG combat phase after a successful jam. One wave vs Door Actuator Daemon.
combat._note: ttl_seconds caps the fight duration; expired sessions count as a loss.

lesson.topic: CTA rail signaling and the elevated Loop
lesson.summary: Chicago's L has circled downtown since 1897. Four CTA lines share the rectangular elevated Loop. Rail signaling uses SCADA block-detection and cab signals — digital control surfaces that can be compromised.
lesson.deepdish_footnote: Educational footnote: always secure your SCADA endpoints, champ.
